_comment: Damodaran FCFF inputs for Marvell Technology (MRVL). Amounts in USD millions.
_data_source: Wisesheets — not yet pulled. Agent must search for TTM financials before running DCF.
_note: MRVL fiscal year ends January. FY2026 = Feb 2025 – Jan 2026.
story_narrative: Marvell is the second wave of AI silicon — the custom ASIC opportunity. Google (TPU), Amazon (Trainium/Inferentia), Microsoft (Maia), and Meta (MTIA) are all designing custom AI chips to reduce dependence on NVIDIA and optimize for their specific workloads. Marvell co-designs these custom ASICs and earns silicon revenue tied to production volume. The hyperscaler custom silicon market could exceed $50B+ by 2028. Marvell also benefits from AI networking demand (AEC copper interconnects, ethernet PHY). The risk: custom ASIC timelines slip, or hyperscalers bring more design work in-house. MRVL is less commodity than memory but more dependent on execution than TSMC.
story_revenue_cagr: 25% reflects rapid ramp of custom ASIC programs (Google TPU volume + Amazon Trainium 2 ramp + new wins). Revenue mix shifting from legacy enterprise/carrier to high-margin AI data center.
story_target_margin: 32% target EBIT margin as AI data center mix grows. Currently lower (~15–20%) due to legacy business drag and R&D investment in new AI programs. Margin expands as AI becomes dominant.
story_growth_years: 10 years — custom ASIC program cycles are 2–3 years each; multiple hyperscaler programs staggered across the period.
story_stable_growth: 3% stable growth. Custom ASIC is not commodity; Marvell maintains design relationships but must re-win each generation.
story_stable_margin: 28% — mature custom ASIC design house margin. Higher than commodity fabless but below pure IP/software model.
story_s2c_high: 2.0x — fabless model, but more R&D intensive than NVIDIA due to custom bespoke design for each hyperscaler. Each ASIC program requires significant upfront engineering.
story_s2c_stable: 2.2x — slight efficiency improvement as standard design platforms are reused across customers.
story_beta: 1.55 — higher beta than TSMC/ASML because Marvell's revenue depends on both hyperscaler CapEx cycles and successful program execution. Less visible than TSMC backlog.
story_rd_cap: 5-year amortization. Custom ASIC IP has a useful life tied to the program cycle (~2–3 years) but cumulative process and EDA tool investments persist longer.
story_uncertainty: High — execution risk on custom ASIC programs, customer concentration (top 3 hyperscalers = >60% of AI revenue), and transition from legacy business adds significant forecast uncertainty.
